name: Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas
oneLine: SPF broken (rejects all senders despite live MX); otherwise hardened

emailAuth.note: v=spf1 -all without includes — but MX records confirm mgovcloud.in is the live mail infrastructure. Outbound mail fails SPF at every receiver.

findings: SPF v=spf1 -all with no includes is broken — outbound mail from @mopng.gov.in fails authentication at every receiver
All 6 hardening headers present (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame, X-Content-Type, Referrer, Permissions)

fingerprints.stack: Nginx reverse proxy + Java/Spring backend, eMudhra DV TLS
Nginx + custom backend with ST01* session cookies

attackPaths.name: SPF bypass → email spoofing → cross-ministry phishing
attackPaths.entry: SPF v=spf1 -all rejects all senders; mgovcloud.in MX is live so legitimate mail fails. Attacker spoofs Secretary/CMD identity from external IPs unchallenged.
attackPaths.pivot: Phishing campaign targeting subordinate ministries; DMARC p=reject blocks recovery for legitimate users; SideCopy-class TTPs documented for HPCL/OISD lures since 2024.
attackPaths.objective: Credential harvest, malware delivery, lateral movement into dghindia.gov.in or pngrb.gov.in.

attackPaths.name: Public dashboard enumeration → sensitive data exposure
attackPaths.entry: dashboard.mopng.gov.in publicly indexed; pariyojana.mopng.gov.in is project-portal subdomain.
attackPaths.pivot: Enumerate Pariyojana API endpoints; ST01* session tokens may be deterministic.
attackPaths.objective: Exfiltrate project status, budget data, stakeholder lists; identify procurement schedules.

attackPaths.name: AWS typosquat cluster → domain-takeover staging
attackPaths.pivot: Pre-stage lookalike with attacker-issued TLS; phishing links to mopng-secure.com.
attackPaths.objective: Harvest credentials, bypass mail filters, persistent backdoor via typosquat MTA.

paths.preAi: 4 hours (SPF audit + recon + domain registration + phishing template)
paths.mythos: 1.5 hours (automated domain generation + header crafting + .gov.in template cache)

paths.preAi: 3 hours (dashboard enumeration + API fuzzing + token analysis)
paths.mythos: 45 min (multi-threaded discovery, JWT pattern cracking, schema-aware filtering)

cisoPatchList.title: Fix broken SPF (currently -all without authorised senders)
cisoPatchList.fix: Replace 'v=spf1 -all' with 'v=spf1 include:mgovcloud.in -all' covering actual mail infrastructure. Audit all MX records. Test 100+ legitimate-sender deliveries before enforcement.
cisoPatchList.validation: mxtoolbox SPF check shows no soft-fail; spoofed mail hard-bounces

cisoPatchList.title: Restrict dashboard.mopng.gov.in to VPN / IP-allowlist
cisoPatchList.fix: Move dashboard off public DNS or restrict access to ministry gateway IPs. Add WAF rules for enumeration probes; rate-limit session-token endpoint.
cisoPatchList.validation: external nmap times out or returns 403; ministry IPs succeed

cisoPatchList.title: Strengthen CSP — add upgrade-insecure-requests + block-all-mixed-content
cisoPatchList.fix: Add 'upgrade-insecure-requests' and 'block-all-mixed-content'. Migrate inline scripts to nonce-based execution.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -i mopng.gov.in | grep CSP shows new directives

cisoPatchList.title: Audit Pariyojana API authentication and rate limiting
cisoPatchList.fix: OAuth 2.0 / API token auth on all endpoints. 100 req/min rate limit per IP/user. HMAC-signed responses.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl without auth returns 401; 101 reqs/min triggers 429

cisoPatchList.title: Implement DNS CAA records to restrict cert issuance
cisoPatchList.fix: Publish: 'CAA 0 issue "emudhra.com"; CAA 0 issuewild "emudhra.com"'. Subscribe to CT monitoring (Censys, certspotter).
cisoPatchList.validation: dig mopng.gov.in CAA shows eMudhra restrictions

cisoPatchList.title: Audit ST01* session-token generation entropy
cisoPatchList.fix: Confirm tokens generated via cryptographic RNG (≥128-bit entropy). Implement 15-min idle / 8-hr absolute timeout.
cisoPatchList.validation: 1000-token entropy check (Shannon ≥7.9 bits/byte); zero collisions over 7-day run

phase2.headlineQuestion: Given the broken SPF and the publicly indexed dashboard subdomain, how is MoPNG currently detecting spoofed emails claiming to be from the ministry, and is Pariyojana project data encrypted at rest?
